name: asset-play
query: Research TMBR (Cascade Timber & Land) using the given facts. The earnings look boring but a fund manager friend says the land is worth more than the company.

given_facts.business: Small, dull timber operator that has owned ~400,000 acres of Pacific Northwest timberland since the 1950s, carried on the books at decades-old cost.
given_facts.appraised_value_of_land_note: comparable timberland sales in the region imply ~1400-1800 for the acreage; carried at ~90 (1950s cost basis)
given_facts.catalyst_note: a new CEO (ex-REIT) hired in 2026; board authorized 'strategic review of land monetization'; an activist holds 8%

expected_behavior: Classifies TMBR as an Asset play: the thesis is the hidden land value (~1400-1800 appraised vs ~90 book vs ~1100 market cap), not the earnings stream.

Computes the per-share math with compute_valuation.py / sourced figures - market cap ~1100 (22 x 50M shares) against the appraised land range - and does NOT value it on P/E or PEG (earnings are beside the point for this category).
Demands a CATALYST before calling the value realizable, and finds it: new ex-REIT CEO, board strategic review, an 8% activist - hidden assets without a catalyst can stay hidden for decades.
Checks the debt is small (80 vs 60 cash) so the asset value isn't mortgaged away - the ch. 15 asset-play debt question.
Notes the Lynch green flags: dull business, low institutional ownership, insider buying.
Is honest about appraisal uncertainty: the 1400-1800 range is from comparable sales, not a transaction; the discount must be large enough to survive a haircut.
Includes a bear case: monetization stalls or is blocked (regulatory/environmental), timber prices fall, the activist leaves, the 'strategic review' produces nothing.
Sets category-correct sell triggers: sell on realization (sale/spin/REIT conversion re-rates the assets) or if the catalyst dies - not on quarterly earnings noise.
Every figure cites a source + as-of date.
